artefact.description: A flagship chart/thesis proposed for the public site: the population converged to near-naked hedging (hedge fraction ~0.00) during the calm 2016-2020 years.
artefact.claimed_finding: The company learns to run naked during calm markets -- a behavioural risk-appetite finding.
artefact.presentation: Chart + narrative, internally coherent, ready to publish.

the_catch: The director held publication ('naked-hedging claim from the site vs hold until I've read the full writeup') and the finding was re-derived: the low hedge fraction traced to a POINT-IN-TIME DATA LEAK in the volatility lookback (future data used in the vol calc). Re-derived blind, the population stays hedged 0.80-0.90 throughout including the calm years. The headline was an artefact of a foresight bug, not a behaviour -- a judge that signs off a finding without checking the epistemic wall on its inputs is a weak judge.
